source: Weyns & Calinescu 2015 [1] Table III (7-service TAS catalogue); topology and variable ranges from __OLD__/data/config/cs1/default_qn_model.csv and __OLD__/data/config/cs1/default_dim_variables.csv.
notes: 13-artifact queueing network, baseline (no adaptation). TAS_1..TAS_6 are composite workflow stages; MAS_1..MAS_3 are Medical Analysis Services; AS_1..AS_3 are Alarm Services; DS_3 is the Drug Service. External arrivals enter at TAS_1 with lambda_z=345 req/s.

c_{TAS_{1}}.description: Number of resources allocated in TAS_1

K_{TAS_{1}}.description: Allocated request capacity in TAS_1 memory buffer

\\delta_{TAS_{1}}.description: Data density for request in TAS_1 expected data per requests

L_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average number of request in TAS_1 component

Lq_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average number of request in TAS_1 memory

W_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average time to complete a request in TAS_1

Wq_{TAS_{1}}.description: Average time for a request to transit the buffer in TAS_1

_labels: No adaptation — TAS workflow as published [1]
environments._note: P[k][i][j] = routing probability from node i to node j under scenario k. Indices follow `_nodes` order. Diagonal captures per-service error probability; off-diagonal captures the TAS workflow routing.
